mission.title: kong: Kong is adding URL encoding before sending upstream
mission.description: Kong is adding URL encoding before sending upstream. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify kong troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kong deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kong -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kong
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kong -n kong --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review kong configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kong configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kong -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kong
kubectl get configmap -n kong -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kong
```
In Kong 0.10.2 it is adding additional URL encoding before sending a request upstream.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Kong is adding URL encoding before sending upstream
steps.description:
### Summary

Since `ngx.req.set_uri` will make Nginx's proxying escape the upstream URI, we do not use this API anymore. As it was the case prior to Kong 0.10, we go back to inlining an Nginx variable containing the desired, plain URI, and thus ensure Nginx will not unconditionally percent-encode
```yaml
Rewrite the current request's (parsed) URI by the uri argument.
```

steps.title: Confirm Kong is adding URL encoding before sending… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kong -n kong --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n kong --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
### Summary

Since `ngx.req.set_uri` will make Nginx's proxying escape the upstream URI, we do not use this API anymore. As it was the case prior to Kong 0.10, we go back to inlining an Nginx variable containing the desired, plain URI, and thus ensure Nginx will not unconditionally percent-encode before proxying.
codeSnippets: Rewrite the current request's (parsed) URI by the uri argument.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kong installed or the issue environment reproducible.
